task_completion.description: Navigate to the WooCommerce Reports section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The WordPress admin dashboard is visible with WooCommerce -> Reports selected.
The 'Sales by Date' tab or view is active within the WooCommerce Reports interface.

task_completion.description: Set the report date range to the previous month.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The date range selector is set to 'Last Month' or shows the start and end dates of the previous calendar month.
The agent clicks the 'Export CSV' button, or a browser download dialog appears for the report.

task_completion.description: Save the exported file with the correct name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, file manager, or terminal window shows the file being named 'last_month_sales.csv'.

task_completion.description: Verify the file is saved in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file 'last_month_sales.csv' is visible inside the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to navigate WooCommerce.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser open to the WordPress admin dashboard, not just terminal commands.

integrity.description: The CSV was genuinely exported from WooCommerce.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent clicks the export button in the WooCommerce interface rather than manually creating a CSV file in a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not manually type the sales data.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the agent manually typing sales figures or dates into a spreadsheet or text file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file path is definitively /home/ga/Documents/last_month_sales.csv. The exported CSV will contain at least 5 rows of data corresponding to synthetic backdated orders distributed across the previous calendar month.
